MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrin Ng, Esq, counsel for and with Marie Phelps, Administrator (appearing separately by video conference); Elizabeth Read, Esq., counsel for Katherine Phelps and Kendall Phelps, Beneficiaries/Objectors (appearing by video conference); Windy Arden, in pro per, Petitioner/Objector (appearing by video conference)

The Court notes the various matters pending in this case, including Ms. Arden's Petitions to Determine Fact of Marriage and a Spousal Property Petition sent to Dept. 206 for trial assignment. Mr. Ng advises the Court that Ms. Arden failed to appear at the trial call and the petitions were dismissed. Ms. Arden responds, advising that she was hospitalized and unable to contact the Court to advise of her absence.

Ms. Read notes that regardless of the want of notice or explanation of Ms. Arden's absence at trial call, she has failed to respond to any discovery.

At the Court's query, Mr. Ng again confirms that the petitions were dismissed at trial call. Ms. Arden advises that she intends to move to have the petitions revived.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission with the intention to grant the pending petition.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Continued To Oct-18-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Read appeared by zoom for and with Kendall Phelps (by zoom) and for Katherine Phelps.
Darrin Ng appeared by zoom for Marie Phelps (Administrator).
Windy Arden appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it is disappointed that it couldn't get a single joint status statement from the parties and asked Ms. Arden about getting an Attorney. Ms. Arden stated that she has been ill, that her means of getting a Lawyer are limited and that she put together a status statement, but she was too late. Ms. Arden also stated that she has a medical procedure scheduled this Friday. The Court noted that Ms. Arden's deposition is scheduled for March 15, 2021 and asked if that was going to work out. Ms. Arden stated that she takes it as it comes, that she needs more time and that the subpoenas have not been returned. Ms. Arden also stated that some discovery is needed, that she has gotten no cooperation and that she got no response to the request for the business records. Mr. Ng stated that his office and his Client received the discovery request, that there were some irregularities and that he asked for clarification. Mr. Ng also stated that they have prepared a response, that they have been working with Ms. Arden to no avail and that there's not a lot to be gained by what is being requested. Ms. Read stated that there has been no discovery request to Katherine or Kendall, that she would like to have this set for trial and that she can proceed without Ms. Arden's deposition. The issue of whether or not Ms. Read will be filing a motion for summary judgment was also raised. The Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage and the Amended Petition for Spousal Property are set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on September 27, 2021 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court). The parties are to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to October 18, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Continued To Feb-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Darrin Ng for and with Marie Phelps.
Elizabeth Read for Katharine Phelps and Kendall Phelps. Kendall Phelps also present via Zoom.
Joseph Barton for and with Windy Arden.

Ms. Read stated Mr. Barton has filed an amended putative spousal petition and she has filed objections, that Mr. Barton and Ms. Arden rejected mediation after settlement was unsuccessful, that there was no wedding in Tahiti, that there was no domestic partnership in CA in 2004 or any other time, and these are implausible claims by the petitioner. Mr. Barton stated that he has been trying to contact Blue Shield the health carrier for the decedent and his wife, that there is a contract regarding domestic relations which is notarized, that he needs 2 subpoenas for records that they are domestic partners and a deposition, and that they need a trial. Ms. Read stated they have heard Mr. Barton say "we need to get subpoenas" since July continuances, and that they believe the law did not allow the Tahitian wedding in 2004. Ms. Arden stated that it's been difficult getting connected to people to get the documents due to the pandemic and people working remotely. The Court stated it will continue the petitions for a joint status report summarizing the claims, disputed facts, witnesses to be called, etc. to be filed by February 8, 2021. Mr. Barton requested the petitions be continued to March to allow enough time to get his deposition and documents. Ms. Read stated the matters need to be moved along and requested the matters be continued to January. The petitions 1.to establish fact of marriage, etc., 2.amended spousal petition and 3.for final distribution are continued to February 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Darin Ng appeared by zoom for and with Marie Phelps by zoom.
Elizabeth Read appeared by zoom for Katharine Phelps and Kendall Phelps.
Joseph Barton appeared by zoom for and with Windy Arden by zoom.

The Court asked Mr. Barton if he has any additional documentary evidence with respect to the fact of marriage. Mr. Barton stated no, that they do not have a marriage certificate and that he also put in a claim of putative spouse. Mr. Barton also stated that they have not subpoenaed any records and that they will need to issue subpoenas because he believes the matter will end up in a trial. The Court stated that if it continues the matter, Mr. Barton will need to act quickly. Ms. Read stated that this has dragged on for a year and a half, that there has been no effort of discovery since the last hearing, that they have a declaration indicating that the Ms. Arden was married at City Hall and that no further evidence has been presented. Mr. Barton stated that their position is that the marriage also involves a marriage in Tahiti, not just in San Francisco. Ms. Read stated that they heard from a Lawyer in Tahiti who has indicated that there is no marriage certificate in Tahiti, and that this matter needs to move forward. The Court asked Mr. Barton how the material submitted constitutes a will. Mr. Barton stated that the document was written when the Decedent was leaving on a trip to Australia. The Court informed Mr. Barton that if he wants to go with forward with the putative spouse claim, he will need to amend the petition. Mr. Barton stated that there are some issues regarding the accounting that will require some discovery and spoke of some life insurance policies. Ms. Read stated that she is not aware of any life insurance policies. Mr. Ng stated that Ms. Arden is not entitled to an accounting. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is denied. The Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage, the Amended Spousal Property Petition and the Petition for Final Distribution are continued to October 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing an amended petition and for Mr. Barton to provide any further evidence. The Court asked everyone to communicate with one another and get this teed up.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2020 Continued To Sep-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Darrin Ng appeared by zoom for and with Marie Phelps (Administrator) by zoom.
Elizabeth Reid appeared by zoom for Katherine Phelps (Mother of the Decedent).
Kendall Phelps (Father of the Decedent) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Joseph Barton appeared by zoom for Windy Arden (Petitioner/Objector).

The Court questions if there are any thoughts on how to resolve this case. Mr. Barton stated that he just substituted in, that he spoke with the Attorney for the Administrator, and that he just introduced himself and that was about it. Mr. Ng stated that he remains neutral on the issue of who the Court determines is entitled to distribution, that his Client's objection is to the appointment of Ms. Arden as Administrator and that is makes no sense to have someone new come in. Ms. Reid stated that there are a number of legal issues that don't require factual findings, that she is not familiar with the Court's pro bono mediation program and that if the Court believes this is the way to proceed, she is amenable. Mr. Ng noted that Ms. Arden failed to file her petition by the deadline. Mr. Barton stated that this case is going to require depositions and subpoenas, and that he is not sure it makes to do mediation without doing discovery. Ms. Reid stated that she believes there are a number of substantive issues that can be disposed of, that they have been to Court three times and that there is no marriage. Mr. Phelps stated that he does not know what mediation will resolve. The Court orders the parties to mediation. Ms. Reid is to contact Elizabeth Kelber and tell her that they have been referred to judicial mediation. The Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage, the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, the Amended Spousal Property Petition, and the Petition for Final Distribution. etc. are continued to September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Apr-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Windy Arden in propria persona.
Darren Ng for and with Administrator Marie Phelps. Also in the courtroom are parents of decedent, Kendall Phelps and Katharine Phelps.

The court inquired if Ms. Arden has counsel. Ms. Arden stated that Mr. Maranto substituted out and she is completely on her own. Mr. Ng stated that they did not get Ms. Arden's filing from Feb. 28th, and that they formally object to the late filed petition.
11:07am - The Court takes a 5 minute recess for Mr. Ng to look at the petition.
11:14am - resumes hearing.
Mr. Ng states decedent's father is also here to object to the petition. The Court noted it does not have enough information to grant the petition for marriage, that the tax returns might be domestic partners but not listed as a spouse, that it needs more proof on marriage, not intent to get married, or proof of domestic partnership filed with the secretary of state. Ms. Arden inquired about the holographic will. The Court noted that it is not testamentary, that there are different ways to interpret "you own everything I am," and that Ms. Arden might want to get an attorney.

The Petition for probate of will and letters testamentary, and Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage are continued to April 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Arden to file and serve by March 30, 2020 and deliver courtesy copies to dept. 202 by April 3, 2020. Ms. Arden to communicate with Mr. Ng on obtaining addresses to serve everyone.

The Accounting petition currently set for March 9, 2020 is continued to April 27, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.
